evidence: src/integrations/supabase/client.ts keeps module-level mutable singleton state (`clientInstance`, `instrumentationInstalled`) and also mutates `window.__supabase_client__`/`window.supabase`, creating hidden runtime coupling and boot-order sensitivity.
src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts stores a process-wide mutable `errorNotifier` configured out-of-band via `configureErrorNotifier`, so behavior depends on external initialization order.

src/features/gallery/hooks/useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts mixes a shared query-key factory with hardcoded query-key tuple matching (`key[0] === 'unified-generations'` etc.), coupling feature logic to cache key internals.

Assigned shared components do not form a coherent capability boundary: AppErrorBoundary is app-shell resilience, AspectRatioSelector is generation settings UI, ActiveLoRAsDisplay is LoRA-domain control; all are placed in one undifferentiated shared/components root.
Assigned shared hooks similarly mix generic infra and domain behavior in one layer (useAsyncOperation vs useApiTokens vs useAutoSaveSettings), reducing predictability of ownership.

Tests for assigned hooks reference divergent package surfaces (e.g., mock '@/shared/lib/errorHandler' and '@/shared/lib/queryKeys') while implementation imports '@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError' and '@/shared/lib/queryKeys/api', making module contracts less trustworthy at subsystem level.

Media lightbox flow has multiple adapter layers that mostly forward fields unchanged: `VideoLightboxProps` -> `useVideoLightboxRenderModel` memo groups -> `LightboxLayout` -> controls (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/VideoLightbox.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx`).

`useButtonGroupProps` builds four prop objects by repeating the same shared fields (`isVideo`, `readOnly`, `isSpecialEditMode`, `selectedProjectId`, `isCloudMode`) with little transformation (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts`).

Task payload contracts rely on very large untyped bags (`Record<string, unknown>`, `UnknownRecord`, nested optional `params.*`) across producers/consumers, reducing interface honesty (`src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts`).

DB-to-domain seam is duplicated: `TaskDbRow` and mapper logic are redefined in both `useTasks` and `generationTaskBridge`, creating drift risk at integration boundaries (`src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts`, `src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts`).

Boundary direction is blurred by exporting `downloadMedia` from `shared/imageEditCore/utils.ts` while sourcing it from a UI component module path, then consuming it from edit hooks (`src/shared/imageEditCore/utils.ts`, `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts`).

Task orchestration handoff depends on implicit key conventions in `params` (`orchestrator_details`, `parent_generation_id`, `run_id`) rather than shared typed contracts, making cross-module integration brittle (`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts`).

`useInlineEditState` (510 lines) and `useImageEditValue` (245 lines) are heavily dominated by manual property wiring and object assembly, which obscures intent and makes local edits error-prone (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts`, `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts`).

`buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` and `buildJoinClipsPayload` construct large conditional objects with many inline branches and key-specific toggles, making edge-case reasoning expensive (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`).

Small wrapper hooks (`useCompletedTimestamp`, `useProcessingTimestamp`, `useUpdatingTimestamp`) add multiple tiny indirection points around the same base hook with limited unique behavior (`src/shared/hooks/useCompletedTimestamp.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useProcessingTimestamp.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useUpdatingTimestamp.ts`).

generateVideoService exposes a broad mixed contract: `buildTravelRequestBody` accepts both grouped params (`motionParams`, `generationParams`, `seedParams`) and legacy flat fields in the same function (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:463-489, 501-520).

Invalidation APIs in one module use mixed semantics for similar operations: synchronous callback style (`useInvalidateGenerations`), fire-and-forget delayed sync (`invalidateGenerationsSync` with internal `setTimeout`), and awaited async refetch (`invalidateVariantChange`) (src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:58-141).

Edge-function helpers expose inconsistent failure contracts: `triggerCostCalculation` swallows/logs errors (supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts:101-130), `checkRateLimit` fails open on RPC failure (supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts:79-121), while `handleStorageOperations` throws on upload failure (supabase/functions/complete_task/storage.ts:68-71).
src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts includes long explanatory block comments for a 1-step wrapper (`getFramePositionForIndex`) that only forwards to shared utilities.
src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts uses repeated large section banners and explanatory prose around straightforward interface fields, creating high comment-to-signal ratio.
src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts repeats extensive migration/context commentary inline with type declarations and constants, making core contracts harder to scan.

src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts keeps canonical types and `TravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput = TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams & Partial<LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput>` active simultaneously.
src/shared/lib/taskIdHelpers.ts still resolves three ID fields (`source_task_id`, `orchestrator_task_id`, legacy `task_id`), preserving legacy shape handling in active logic.

src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts defines `LegacyProjectImageSettings` with deprecated fields and a sunset note, but ingestion still accepts merged input (`ProjectImageSettingsInput`).

holistic_context.structure.root_files marks all 5 assigned root configs as peripheral with low fan-in (vitest.edge.smoke.config.ts fan_in 1; tailwind.config.ts/vite.config.ts/vitest.config.ts/vitest.edge.config.ts fan_in 0), matching straggler-root threshold (<5).

src/shared/components/ has file_count 78 (>10 mixed-concern threshold), avg_fan_in 2.8, and includes assigned files ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx, AppErrorBoundary.tsx, AspectRatioSelector.tsx mixed with many unrelated modals/forms/util-like files.

src/shared/hooks/ has file_count 95 (>10 mixed-concern threshold), avg_fan_in 6.1, and includes assigned hooks with divergent responsibilities (auth token CRUD, generic async wrapper, autosave state machine).
useAutoSaveSettings.ts is 662 LOC inside the flat shared hook bucket, increasing navigational ambiguity versus a focused persistence/settings package.

Large orchestration hooks aggregate many unrelated responsibilities and pass-through state/action bags (e.g., `useImageEditOrchestrator` is 541 LOC with media state, mode orchestration, persistence sync, and generation handlers in one hook).

`useGenerationController` and `useEditingController` each take very wide parameter objects and mostly forward subsets to nested hooks, indicating control-layer indirection without strong local behavior compression.
Lightbox flow uses multiple adapter layers (`MediaLightbox` prop bundling -> `ImageLightbox`/`VideoLightbox` -> `useLightboxWorkflowProps`) that repeatedly reshape the same workflow/action data.

Complete-task child generation path combines extraction/migration compatibility logic, branching behavior for single-item vs multi-item flows, duplicate detection, variant creation, and persistence in one file (`generation-child.ts`), with additional fallback path logic in `params.ts`.

UI primitives use a distinct formatting/export island: `src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/label.tsx`, and `src/shared/components/ui/sonner.tsx` consistently use double quotes and no semicolons, while neighboring shared modules in this batch (e.g. `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts`, `src/app/routes.tsx`) use single quotes and semicolons.
The outlier pattern is concentrated in one sibling UI area and not consistently applied across other shared components/hooks in the assigned set, increasing local style drift and review friction.

Mixed failure signaling appears within task/data modules: `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`, and `src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts` catch, call `handleError(...)`, then rethrow; but `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` logs and returns `null` on query failures (`findSiblingSegments`, `checkFinalStitchStatus`) which can silently suppress hard failures.

In `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`, most Supabase errors are wrapped into new `Error(...)`, but variant insert throws raw `variantError` after `console.error`, creating inconsistent error types/context versus the shared normalized `handleError` path used elsewhere.

Local debug logging plus mixed throw/null behavior in `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` and `src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts` further fragments error handling conventions.

Auth gating strategy is mixed across assigned files: `src/app/routes.tsx` uses `hasStoredSessionToken()` from `src/shared/lib/supabaseSession.ts` (localStorage token presence) for redirect decisions, while edge function `supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` enforces `authenticateRequest(...)` and service-role checks.

Client-side session checks are documented as non-authoritative in routing comments, but they still influence navigation behavior, creating a separate trust-path from stricter request-time auth validation.
No direct service-role misuse was found in assigned frontend files; privileged key usage is confined to edge function code in this batch.

findings.summary: Global mutable singletons create hidden cross-module initialization dependencies.
evidence: Supabase client module persists mutable singleton state and writes debug globals on `window`, so import/use order influences runtime behavior.
Error handling module keeps a process-wide `errorNotifier` that must be configured elsewhere before toasts can work.
Toaster wiring relies on shared `toastManager`/`toast` exports, reinforcing globally shared mutable UI state instead of explicit dependency flow.
findings.suggestion: Create an explicit app bootstrap/composition root that instantiates Supabase, error notifier, and toast services once, inject them through providers/context, and keep shared libs pure by removing mutable module-level registries where possible.

findings.summary: Gallery preloader depends on React Query key internals instead of a stable boundary.
evidence: Preloader cleanup inspects raw query key positions and string literals (`key[0]`, `key[1]`, `key[2]`) instead of using a shared key parser/predicate API.
The same feature area already centralizes behavior behind hooks, but this cache-shape knowledge bypasses those boundaries and increases ripple risk when key schema changes.
findings.suggestion: Add shared query-key predicates/helpers (e.g., `isProjectGenerationsKey`, `extractProjectGenerationsPage`) in the query-keys module and switch cleanup logic to those helpers so key shape changes are localized.

findings.summary: Mobile interaction behavior is spread across unrelated shared modules with overlapping policies.
evidence: Device/touch detection exists in `useMobile.tsx`, while tooltip touch-open/close policy is independently reimplemented in `TouchableTooltip`.
Media gallery separately implements tap timing, double-tap semantics, and outside-click closure, duplicating interaction policy at feature level.
Home pane adds another mobile-specific tooltip state machine (`ecosystemTipOpen`/`ecosystemTipDisabled`) instead of using a unified interaction abstraction.
findings.suggestion: Define one shared mobile interaction contract (e.g., a `useTouchOverlayBehavior` or interaction policy service) and migrate tooltip/gallery/home consumers to that contract so gesture timing and open/close semantics are owned in one place.

findings.summary: Media gallery orchestration hooks blend multiple responsibilities and blur ownership.
evidence: `useMediaGalleryHandlers` coordinates navigation, context-driven shot switching, filter mutations, modal state transitions, and error reporting in one boundary.

`useMediaGalleryItemState` accumulates unrelated local concerns (star state, drag state, selector state, modal lifecycle, async create-shot handling), while `ShotActions` also embeds navigation/addition branching tied to the same flows.
findings.suggestion: Split gallery logic into explicit domain controllers (shot targeting, detail modal flow, item UI state) with narrow hook interfaces, then keep components as thin render layers over those controllers.

findings.summary: MediaLightbox introduces a high-indirection prop/model chain with limited transformation value.
evidence: `VideoLightboxContent` builds mode/environment/shared/edit/render models, then passes `renderModel.layoutProps` into `LightboxLayout` (`VideoLightbox.tsx`).
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` builds multiple memoized group objects (`lightboxCore`, `lightboxMedia`, `lightboxVariants`, `lightboxNavigation`) that mostly forward existing fields.
`useButtonGroupProps` duplicates shared context fields into four returned objects with almost no behavioral translation.
findings.suggestion: Define one explicit view-model contract per layout mode and remove pass-through builders; let downstream controls consume smaller focused slices directly from that contract.

findings.summary: Task DB row normalization is duplicated across modules, creating integration drift risk.
evidence: Both files define their own `TaskDbRow` with near-identical shape and separate mappers (`mapDbTaskToTask` vs `mapDbTaskRowToTask`).
Both mappers perform identical snake_case -> camelCase conversion and `params` object guarding.
findings.suggestion: Extract a single shared task-row codec/mapper module and reuse it from both hooks/bridge code; enforce with type tests to keep DB-schema alignment.

findings.summary: Task creation/completion contract relies on large untyped parameter bags and implicit key conventions.
evidence: `individualTravelSegment` uses `originalParams?: Record<string, unknown>`, multiple `UnknownRecord` helpers, and key probing logic.
`payloadBuilder` returns `Record<string, unknown>` with many conditionally injected keys and legacy compatibility fields.
`complete_task/generation.ts` and `orchestrator.ts` route behavior via optional `params.*` and `params.orchestrator_details.*` lookups.
findings.suggestion: Introduce versioned shared task payload schemas (e.g., zod/io-ts types) used by both creators and complete_task handlers; validate and narrow once at boundary entry.

findings.summary: Core edit utils re-export from component-level module path, obscuring true dependency direction.
evidence: `src/shared/imageEditCore/utils.ts` only re-exports `downloadMedia` from `@/shared/components/MediaLightbox/utils`.
`useInlineEditState` imports `downloadMedia` from `@/shared/imageEditCore/utils`, hiding that image-edit core depends on media-lightbox component utilities.
findings.suggestion: Move `downloadMedia` to a neutral shared lib module (e.g., `src/shared/lib/media/download.ts`) and make both image-edit and lightbox depend on that lower-level utility.

findings.summary: Inline edit state assembly is dominated by very large manual wiring blocks, reducing local clarity.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` composes many sub-hooks and returns a deeply expanded object (`InlineEditStateResult`) with extensive field-by-field mapping.
`buildImageEditValueParams` and `buildImageEditState` are primarily large pass-through object mergers over a massive params interface.
`InlineEditView` depends on this broad state object for both mobile and desktop branches, increasing accidental coupling.
findings.suggestion: Split edit state into 2-3 focused domains (canvas/annotation, generation actions, transform) with dedicated hooks/context selectors, and expose smaller contracts to `InlineEditView`.

findings.summary: A single request-builder API accepts both modern grouped inputs and legacy flat fields, creating ambiguous precedence and hard-to-test call shapes.
evidence: `buildTravelRequestBody` takes grouped objects (`motionParams`, `generationParams`, `seedParams`) while also accepting 16+ legacy flat fields, then conditionally synthesizes grouped params when absent (generateVideoService.ts:463-520).

The same module exports many internals as public/test APIs (`_buildTravelRequestBody`, `_buildStructureGuidance`, etc.), which broadens and fragments the callable surface (generateVideoService.ts:596-612).

Transformer module centralizes conversions with stricter single-shape APIs (`transformGeneration`, `transformVariant`) by contrast, highlighting inconsistency in adjacent request/transform seams (generationTransformers.ts:262-472).
findings.suggestion: Split into explicit `buildTravelRequestBodyV2(params)` and a thin deprecated adapter for legacy callers; remove mixed optional flat fields from the primary signature and keep test coverage against only one canonical input shape.

findings.summary: Form/editor handoff relies on spread-flattening large prop groups, making boundary ownership implicit and collision-prone.
evidence: `ShotImagesEditorResolvedProps` merges four large interfaces and runtime-resolves by spread order (`displayOptions`, `imageState`, `editActions`, `shotWorkflow`) (ShotImagesEditor/types.ts:168-180).
`JoinClipsSettingsFormResolvedProps` repeats the same flattening pattern across clip/motion/UI sections (JoinClipsSettingsForm/types.ts:101-112).
These flattening seams have no collision guards, so similarly named fields across sections silently override by order.
findings.suggestion: Keep sectioned props at runtime (no flatten helper), or enforce a namespaced resolved shape (`{display, image, edit, workflow}`) so ownership is explicit and collisions are impossible.

findings.summary: Invalidation helpers for the same domain mix fire-and-forget, delayed side effects, and awaited refetch semantics without a consistent contract.
evidence: `useInvalidateGenerations` returns a callback that may schedule async timeout work and returns `void` (useGenerationInvalidation.ts:62-81).
`invalidateGenerationsSync` also returns `void` but can defer invalidation via `setTimeout`, so 'sync' is behaviorally misleading (useGenerationInvalidation.ts:84-97).
`invalidateVariantChange` is `async` and performs awaited refetch for some keys but non-awaited invalidations for others (useGenerationInvalidation.ts:105-141).
Timeline writes expose explicit serialized async semantics with typed timeout errors, showing a stricter seam style that invalidation does not match (timelineWriteQueue.ts:54-190).
findings.suggestion: Standardize invalidation APIs to either always return `Promise<void>` (awaitable completion) or clearly split `schedule*` vs `flush*` operations; rename misleading sync variants and document completion guarantees.

findings.summary: Shared edge helpers use inconsistent error contracts (throw vs fail-open vs swallow), making caller behavior unpredictable across adjacent boundaries.
evidence: `triggerCostCalculation` catches and only logs all failures, never signaling failure to caller (billing.ts:101-130).
`checkRateLimit` intentionally allows requests on RPC/runtime errors and returns synthetic success metadata (rateLimit.ts:79-121).
`handleStorageOperations` throws on upload failure and forces caller exception handling path (storage.ts:68-71).
findings.suggestion: Adopt one explicit shared result pattern for edge helpers (e.g., `{ok, error, data}`) with documented fail-open/fail-closed policy per helper, and require callers to branch on result rather than implicit throw/log behavior.

findings.summary: Storage URL helper implies existence checking but only constructs public URLs, weakening boundary honesty for upstream orchestration.
evidence: `getStoragePublicUrl` comment states no existence verification, but return type includes `exists` boolean and sets `exists: true` when URL is constructed (storage.ts:176-193).

Other shared helpers in the same edge layer are used as decision boundaries for workflow/billing actions, so misleading status fields can propagate wrong orchestration assumptions (billing.ts utilities are used for task relationship control flow; billing.ts:19-87).
findings.suggestion: Rename to `buildStoragePublicUrl` and remove `exists`, or add a real existence probe (e.g., `list`/`download` head check) before returning `exists: true`.

findings.summary: High-density restating comments and banners reduce signal-to-noise in otherwise simple modules.
evidence: image-utils.ts documents obvious mechanics at length around a pass-through wrapper (`extractExistingFrames` + `calculateFrameForIndex`).
travelBetweenImages/types.ts repeats large separator blocks and prose for basic interface grouping, inflating file verbosity.
ImageGenerationForm/types.ts contains extensive inline migration narrative mixed with routine type declarations.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to non-obvious invariants and migration constraints only; remove section-banner boilerplate and restating prose. Keep concise doc comments only where behavior or contract is surprising.

findings.summary: Canonical and legacy payload fields are still co-accepted, extending migration debt and ambiguity.
evidence: Travel task ingestion type explicitly unions canonical params with `Partial<LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput>`.
Task ID extraction still falls back to legacy `task_id` after canonical fields.
Project image settings input still allows deprecated legacy fields through a merged input type despite a stated sunset contract.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a strict migration boundary: decode legacy fields once at ingress, emit only canonical shapes downstream, add telemetry for legacy-field usage, and remove legacy fallbacks once usage reaches zero before the stated sunset date.

findings.summary: Assigned components are placed in an overloaded flat shared/components directory that mixes unrelated domains.
evidence: Directory profile for src/shared/components/: file_count 78, avg_fan_in 2.8, avg_fan_out 4.3 (mixed concerns beyond >10 threshold).

ActiveLoRAsDisplay is LoRA-specific generation UI, AppErrorBoundary is app-shell fault isolation, and AspectRatioSelector is settings control; they represent distinct ownership boundaries but share one top-level folder.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create target folders `src/shared/components/lora/`, `src/app/components/boundaries/`, and `src/shared/components/settings/`; (2) move files in order: ActiveLoRAsDisplay -> lora, AspectRatioSelector -> settings, AppErrorBoundary -> app boundary; (3) update imports with `rg -n "@/shared/components/(ActiveLoRAsDisplay|AspectRatioSelector|AppErrorBoundary)" src` then replace paths; (4) validate with `pnpm vitest -c vitest.config.ts` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Assigned hooks sit in a very large shared/hooks bucket that blurs package boundaries between infrastructure and feature persistence.
evidence: Directory profile for src/shared/hooks/: file_count 95 (>10 threshold), total_loc 17634, broad dependency spread (imports from lib, supabase, errorHandling, queryKeys, contexts).

useAsyncOperation is generic execution infra, useApiTokens is API-token domain logic, and useAutoSaveSettings is persistence/state-orchestration (662 LOC), indicating package-affinity mismatch inside one folder.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create `src/shared/hooks/async/`, `src/shared/hooks/auth/`, `src/shared/hooks/settings/`; (2) move in order: useAsyncOperation -> async, useApiTokens -> auth, useAutoSaveSettings -> settings; (3) move tests in lockstep under matching `__tests__` subfolders; (4) update imports via `rg -n "use(ApiTokens|AsyncOperation|AutoSaveSettings)" src`; (5) validate with `pnpm vitest -c vitest.config.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Assigned hook tests mock older/broader module surfaces than the implementations actually import, weakening architectural contract clarity.
evidence: useApiTokens.ts imports `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError` and `@/shared/lib/queryKeys/api`, while its test mocks `@/shared/lib/errorHandler` and `@/shared/lib/queryKeys`.
useAutoSaveSettings.ts imports `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError`, while its test mocks `@/shared/lib/errorHandler`.
This mismatch signals unclear canonical package surfaces for error/query utilities.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) define canonical module surfaces (either direct leaf imports or explicit barrels under `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/index.ts` and `src/shared/lib/queryKeys/index.ts`); (2) update implementations and tests to one convention in order: useApiTokens + test, then useAutoSaveSettings + test, then useAsyncOperation + test for consistency sweep; (3) run `rg -n "errorHandler|errorHandling/handleError|lib/queryKeys(/api)?" src/shared/hooks` to verify convergence; (4) validate with `pnpm vitest -c vitest.config.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useApiTokens.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAutoSaveSettings.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAsyncOperation.test.ts`.

findings.summary: Root-level config files are all low-fan-in peripherals; heavy config logic should be extracted behind focused config packages with thin root entrypoints.
evidence: holistic root_files: all five assigned root configs are role=peripheral with fan_in 0-1 (<5 straggler threshold).
tailwind.config.ts is 743 LOC at repo root, while vitest edge configs are already thin delegators to `supabase/functions/*`, showing partial extraction pattern.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create `config/build/` and `config/test/`; (2) extract substantive objects/functions from `tailwind.config.ts`, `vite.config.ts`, `vitest.config.ts` into `config/*` modules; (3) keep required root filenames as thin wrappers exporting imported config; (4) update internal imports with `rg -n "from './(tailwind|vite|vitest)"`; (5) validate via `pnpm vite build`, `pnpm vitest -c vitest.config.ts`, and `pnpm vitest -c vitest.edge.config.ts`.

findings.summary: Global singleton/notifier contracts create implicit cross-module runtime dependencies.
evidence: `handleError.ts` behavior changes globally after `configureErrorNotifier(...)`, but callers of `handleError(...)` cannot see that dependency from the function contract.

`client.ts` keeps legacy imports working via proxy while also mutating global/window state (`window.__supabase_client__`, `window.supabase`), which creates hidden architectural coupling to browser runtime and init timing.
These modules are top import hubs in holistic context, so implicit contracts propagate widely.
findings.suggestion: Define explicit contracts and ownership boundaries: (1) introduce `src/app/bootstrap/errorNotifier.ts` to register notifier once at app startup and keep `handleError` pure with injected notifier in call sites that need UI effects; (2) split `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` into `clientFactory.ts` (creation/runtime init), `clientSingleton.ts` (lifecycle), and `legacyProxy.ts` (temporary compatibility export); (3) add a short “runtime contracts” section in README documenting required bootstrap order and temporary compatibility layers; validate with `npm run typecheck && npm run test` and dependency checks via `rg -n "configureErrorNotifier|getSupabaseClient|import \{ supabase \}" src`.

findings.summary: Top-level docs omit runtime architecture contracts that materially affect behavior.
evidence: `README.md` links architecture docs but does not define compatibility/runtime invariants for error notification wiring or Supabase lazy singleton/proxy behavior.
`handleError.ts` includes app/UI registration semantics despite being in shared lib, so architectural ownership is not obvious from package role alone.
`client.ts` includes backward-compat and debug-global behavior, but this contract is not surfaced at top-level project contract documentation.
findings.suggestion: Add a governance-level contract section in `README.md` (or linked structure doc) that explicitly states: error notifier registration point, Supabase client singleton ownership, compatibility proxy sunset criteria, and permitted import surface (`getSupabaseClient` preferred over proxy). Pair that doc update with a small architectural decision record under docs and enforce with a CI grep check like `rg -n "import \{ supabase \}" src | wc -l` to track legacy proxy usage burn-down.

findings.summary: Flat `shared/lib` plus generic `utils.ts` barrel obscures ownership and promotes broad coupling.
evidence: `src/shared/lib/` has 71 production files with mixed concerns (objective overload threshold exceeded).
`utils.ts` exports unrelated helpers (`cn`, `cropFilename`, `truncateText`) behind a generic path, and high fan-in indicates it is a dependency funnel.
`button.tsx` depends on `@/shared/lib/utils` only for `cn`, showing UI styling utility consumption through a broad shared-lib barrel instead of focused package ownership.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 target folders: create `src/shared/lib/ui/` for UI class helpers and `src/shared/lib/text/` for string formatting; move `uiClassNames.ts -> ui/cn.ts` and `stringFormatting.ts -> text/stringFormatting.ts`. Stage 2 move order: update `src/shared/lib/utils.ts` to re-export from new locations temporarily, then migrate direct imports in high-traffic UI files (start with `src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx` and sibling UI components), then remove mixed barrel exports. Stage 3 tighten package boundaries: keep error handling isolated under `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/` and prefer domain barrels like `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` only within type domains. Import-update/validation commands: `rg -n "@/shared/lib/utils" src`, apply replacements to `@/shared/lib/ui/cn` or `@/shared/lib/text/stringFormatting`, then run `npm run typecheck && npm run test` and `rg -n "@/shared/lib/utils" src` to ensure barrel usage is intentionally minimized.

findings.summary: Controller hooks rely on oversized prop bags and pass-through chaining instead of focused boundaries
evidence: `useImageEditOrchestrator` defines a very large input contract and composes multiple mode systems plus persistence and generation actions in one unit.
`useGenerationController` accepts many generation/join/timeline settings and forwards most of them into `useGenerateBatch`, reducing local decision value.
`useEditingController` aggregates structure-video, audio, name-editing, and join-segment setup/handlers and returns a broad merged surface.
`useLightboxWorkflowProps` rebuilds workflow objects by reshaping existing grouped props, adding another translation layer.
findings.suggestion: Split controller boundaries by workflow intent (editing, generation, navigation) and replace broad prop bags with narrower domain-specific inputs; let each controller own a small decision surface and expose only stable command-style outputs.

findings.summary: Lightbox split architecture still duplicates data-shaping patterns across wrappers
evidence: `MediaLightbox` builds grouped `navigation/shotWorkflow/features/actions/videoProps` from a flat prop surface, then re-passes these into both specialized lightboxes.
`ImageLightbox` and `VideoLightbox` each add additional composition layers before reaching `LightboxLayout`, producing repeated adaptation logic.
`useLightboxWorkflowProps` again maps grouped inputs into workflow bar/control/layout props, creating multiple near-sequential shape transformations.
findings.suggestion: Define a single canonical lightbox view-model builder (shared contract + normalization point) and pass that model through image/video renderers directly; remove intermediate reshaping layers unless they add behavior.

findings.summary: Child-generation completion code mixes migration compatibility, orchestration parsing, and persistence workflows
evidence: `handleChildGeneration` mutates incoming params, branches on single-item semantics, extracts nested orchestrator fields, checks existing generations, creates variants, performs self-heal updates, and falls through to record creation.

`params.ts` contains wide fallback extraction logic for many historical param shapes and also includes write-path behavior helpers (thumbnail path config/building), coupling migration compatibility with current domain operations.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit phases for `normalize input -> decide strategy -> persist outcome`; isolate legacy-path extraction into a dedicated normalization module with typed output consumed by smaller creation/variant services.

findings.summary: Image generation form submission path uses low-leverage wrapper layering
evidence: `useFormSubmission` primarily destructures and forwards a large prop set to `useTaskParamsBuilder` and `useSubmissionHandlers`.
`useSubmissionHandlers` mostly rethreads the same inputs into `useSubmitHandler` and `usePromptQueueHandlers` with minimal local orchestration.
Queue handler logic and submit handler composition are separated, but the current layering still requires repeated long argument lists across adjacent hooks.
findings.suggestion: Collapse wrapper layers into a single submission coordinator with internal modules for `task param build`, `queueing`, and `submit`; share a compact context object rather than repeatedly threading near-identical argument lists.

findings.summary: UI primitive siblings follow a distinct quote/semicolon convention that diverges from broader shared-module style.
evidence: All four UI files use double-quoted imports/strings and omit semicolons.
Non-UI shared files in this batch (e.g. `handleError.ts`, `routes.tsx`) use single quotes and semicolons.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one enforced formatter/lint profile for `src/shared/components/ui/*` and the broader shared codebase (or explicitly scope an override) to eliminate style-island drift.

findings.summary: Generation mutation helpers mix wrapped `Error` throws, raw backend throws, and direct console logging.
evidence: `useGenerationMutations.ts` wraps most failures as `new Error(...)` but throws raw `variantError` for variant creation after `console.error`.
Other modules in the same batch route failures through `handleError(...)` normalization and contextual logging.
findings.suggestion: Normalize all mutation failures to a single error contract (prefer `handleError` normalization + contextual metadata) and remove ad-hoc `console.error` branches in favor of one reporting path.

findings.summary: Orchestrator completion helpers convert some DB errors into `null` returns, risking silent partial failure.
evidence: `orchestrator.ts` logs and returns `null` in `findSiblingSegments(...)` and returns `'not_found'` on final-step query error, while caller paths often just return early.
Other task modules in this batch (`imageGeneration.ts`) catch, call `handleError`, and rethrow to preserve failure semantics.
findings.suggestion: Use explicit typed failure results or throw with structured context for orchestrator query failures, and make callers handle hard-failure vs not-found as separate branches.

findings.summary: Route redirect decisions rely on local token presence while other code paths enforce request-time auth, creating inconsistent trust boundaries.
evidence: `authRedirectLoader` redirects based on `hasStoredSessionToken()` from localStorage.
`calculate-task-cost` enforces `authenticateRequest(...)` plus `auth.isServiceRole` before processing.
findings.suggestion: Keep localStorage checks as performance hints only and align route-gating logic with an authoritative session check abstraction (or explicitly isolate the fast-path and centralize final authorization gating in one shared policy).

findings.summary: Tool package keeps long-lived compatibility re-exports that blur shared/tool boundaries
evidence: Tool-local files are pure compatibility aliases to shared components/types, preserving historical import paths instead of converging on one boundary.
settings.ts re-exports shared phase config while simultaneously holding tool-local schema, making ownership split ambiguous.
findings.suggestion: Create a single explicit compatibility surface (for example `src/tools/travel-between-images/compat/`) and migrate call sites to shared canonical imports first; after import migration, delete tool-level alias files and block new aliases with a lint rule (`no-restricted-imports` for deprecated paths).

findings.summary: Complete-task generation helpers mix null-sentinel and throwing error styles
evidence: generation-core has helpers that swallow and return null for failures, while other helpers throw new Error on DB failures.
generation-parent predominantly returns null on failures, but generation.ts catches and rethrows in outer routing.
findings.suggestion: Standardize generation helpers on one contract (prefer `Result<T, E>` or throw-only) and adapt call sites once at router boundaries; include typed error categories so upstream can map to stable HTTP responses without branchy null checks.

findings.summary: Several abstraction layers mostly reshape and forward data without real leverage
evidence: useFormContextBuilder spends most logic on typed pass-through assembly via Pick/ReturnType and memo wrappers.
MediaLightbox `useBundledLightboxProps` repackages one large prop object into grouped bags, but leaves semantics mostly unchanged.
findings.suggestion: Collapse pass-through builders where possible: expose narrower context selectors directly from source hooks, and split MediaLightbox into explicit `ImageLightboxContainer` / `VideoLightboxContainer` APIs with only mode-relevant props.

findings.summary: Large multi-mode interfaces make behavior and usage contracts hard to predict
evidence: MediaLightboxProps combines many optional concerns (navigation, editing, shot ops, task details, video mode).
Shot editor controller boundary carries broad callback/config payloads that reflect multiple responsibilities in one API.
findings.suggestion: Replace broad optional interfaces with scenario-specific contracts: split by mode/workflow, then compose through a thin parent coordinator. Add compile-time guard types so each mode receives only valid parameter sets.

findings.summary: Legacy/new schema paths coexist in active runtime flows
evidence: Segment settings support multiple deprecated metadata shapes and legacy user_overrides in active code paths.
ImageGenerationForm still runs one-time legacy migration hooks in runtime rather than having fully retired data formats.
findings.suggestion: Run a staged migration retirement: (1) add telemetry counters for legacy field reads/writes, (2) backfill remaining data and freeze legacy writes, (3) remove legacy read branches and compatibility types after observed zero usage for a release window.

findings.summary: Large flat directories plus alias entrypoints reduce ownership clarity
evidence: Structure profile: `src/shared/components/` has 78 files and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/` has high average fan-out (10.2), indicating mixed concerns in broad folders.
Duplicate basename entrypoint pattern (file + folder implementation) appears in multiple component areas, making canonical location less obvious.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 target folders: create `src/shared/components/feature/lightbox/` and `src/shared/components/feature/phase-config/`, plus `src/tools/travel-between-images/compat/` for temporary aliases only. Stage 2 move order: move implementation files first (`MediaLightbox/*`, `PhaseConfigSelectorModal/*`), then update direct imports, then relocate remaining alias shims into `compat/`. Stage 3 import updates: run `rg -l "@/shared/components/MediaLightbox|@/shared/components/PhaseConfigSelectorModal|@/tools/travel-between-images/components/PhaseConfigVertical|@/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/InlineSegmentVideo" src | xargs sed -i '' -e 's#old#new#g'` with explicit per-path replacements. Stage 4 validation commands: `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit`, `pnpm -s vitest run`, `pnpm -s eslint src --max-warnings=0`.

findings.summary: Shot editor controller centralizes too many responsibilities in one orchestration hook
evidence: useShotEditorController (826 LOC) coordinates state, settings, navigation, data fetching, refs, and feature-mode behavior in one unit.
Downstream controllers return broad bundles, keeping high cognitive load at the top-level orchestrator.
findings.suggestion: Refactor into a layered model: `useShotEditorModel` (pure derived state), `useShotEditorActions` (mutations/commands), and view-mode-specific controller hooks (`timeline`, `batch`, `join`) consumed by a thin shell. Keep each layer with explicit input/output contracts and local tests.
